claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, their eligibility for Child Benefit depends on their current situation. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or NVQs up to level 3) or on an approved training course?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in sixth form doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know, thank you.

Since Alex is 18 and studying A-levels in sixth form, there's one more thing I need to check regarding the education requirement.

**When did Alex start their A-level course, and when is it expected to finish?** 

Also, **did Alex enrol on or start this course before their 19th birthday?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex started the A-levels last September, so yes, well before turning 19. The course is expected to finish next summer - so it's a standard two-year A-level program.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for those details. That's very helpful.

I have a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex, meaning do they live with you and do you contribute to the costs of their upbringing?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home, and I support them - I pay for everything they need really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's great.

Just a couple more questions to complete my assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK?** And also, **is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- You live in the UK
- Alex is 18 years old and lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex (they live with you and you support them financially)
- Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form)
- Alex started this course before turning 19
- The course will finish next summer (within the eligible timeframe)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 18):**
- ✅ Although Alex is over 16, Child Benefit can continue until age 20 for children in approved education or training
- ✅ A-levels at sixth form counts as approved non-advanced education
- ✅ Alex started the course before turning 19
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex (they live with you and you support them)
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You'll be able to continue claiming Child Benefit for Alex until they finish their A-levels next summer. Just be aware that you'll need to inform the Child Benefit Office when Alex finishes their education or if their circumstances change (for example, if they start working 24 or more hours per week).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form, which qualifies as approved non-advanced education. Alex started this course before turning 19, and the course will finish next summer (within the eligible period). The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (Alex lives with them and they support Alex financially), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. Although Alex is 18 years old, they remain eligible because Alex is in approved full-time education (A-levels), started the course before turning 19, and the claimant meets all other requirements: living in the UK, being responsible for the child, and no duplicate claims exist.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form, which qualifies as approved non-advanced education. Alex started this course before turning 19, and the course will finish next summer (within the eligible period). The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (Alex lives with them and they support Alex financially), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. Although Alex is 18 years old, they remain eligible because Alex is in approved full-time education (A-levels), started the course before turning 19, and the claimant meets all other requirements: living in the UK, being responsible for the child, and no duplicate claims exist.
